basic_details.job_title: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 110 Peon, Driver, Steno and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Bengaluru City Civil Court has officially released two recruitment notifications - No. Aa.Vi.1(9)/429/2026 and No. Aa.Vi.1(9)/428/2026 - dated 24 June 2026, inviting eligible candidates from Karnataka (371-B) to apply for 110 total vacancies across posts including Typist Grade-III, Peon, Driver, Process Server, and Peon (Javana). Applications must be submitted online only through the official portal, with the last date being 30 July 2026. Both notifications are subject to the final order of the Hon'ble Karnataka High Court in Writ Petition Nos. 200448/2025, 17968/2025, and 3145/2023.

application_fee.details: Payment Method: Online only - after submitting the application, pay through the SBI Collect page using Net Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card. | Important: Fee must be paid in the name of "Vitteeshadhikariyavaru, Nagar Civil Nyayalaya, Bengaluru" only. Fee paid in any other name will not be accepted. Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be at least 18 years old and must not exceed the maximum age on the last date of application. | Category Maximum Age General Merit 40 years Category-II(A), II(B), III(A), III(B) 43 years Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, Category-I 45 years | Age Relaxation: | Government servants (State/Central/Public Sector) serving for at least 8 of the last 10 years in a lower post are eligible for relaxation up to the prescribed upper age limit. Ex-Servicemen: 3 additional years on top of military service period. Physically disabled candidates: 10 years relaxation. Widows: 10 years relaxation. As per Government Order No. SAASUI 262, Sec. 2025, dated 29.01.2026, 05 additional years are added to the upper age limit.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about the educational requirements can refer to the post-wise breakdown below. | Candidates must be at least 18 years old and must not exceed the maximum age on the last date of application. | Age Relaxation: | Government servants (State/Central/Public Sector) serving for at least 8 of the last 10 years in a lower post are eligible for relaxation up to the prescribed upper age limit. Ex-Servicemen: 3 additional years on top of military service period. Physically disabled candidates: 10 years relaxation. Widows: 10 years relaxation. As per Government Order No. SAASUI 262, Sec. 2025, dated 29.01.2026, 05 additional years are added to the upper age limit.

selection_process: The selection process for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026 is merit-based and includes a written test followed by a skill/interview round. Candidates should prepare early as competition will be high given the large number of applicants expected.

Written Test - Based on marks scored in the qualifying educational examination Typing Test - Type a passage of 120 words in 1 minute in both Kannada and English; transcribe it and type at 45 WPM (minimum 50 marks required to qualify); maximum 100 marks Interview / Oral Test - Marks scored in the interview round Final merit list based on aggregate of all three components
Written Test - Based on qualifying exam marks Typing Test - Type a passage in Kannada and English (maximum 100 marks; minimum 50 marks to qualify) Interview / Oral Test Final merit based on aggregate
Eligible candidates (50 marks in qualifying exam) called for driving test Shortlisting ratio: 1:25 per vacancy based on qualifying exam marks Interview carries 10 marks
Shortlisting ratio: 1:25 per vacancy based on SSLC marks Interview carries 10 marks
Shortlisting ratio: 1:10 per vacancy based on SSLC marks Interview carries 10 marks
Probation Period: All selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years from the date of joining
Tie-breaking rule: If two or more candidates score equally
seniority in age (older person ranks higher) and the more qualified candidate ranks above less qualified.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply exclusively through the online portal. No physical or offline applications will be accepted. Here is a clear step-by-step process:
Visit the official recruitment portal: Click Here
Read the full notification carefully before filling the form.
Register with a valid email ID and mobile number (these will be used for all future communication).
Fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details.
Upload required documents - self-attested scanned copies of certificates and photographs.
Pay the application fee online via SBI Collect (Net Banking / Debit / Credit Card). SC/ST/Cat-I/PwD candidates are fee-exempt.
Do not upload fee payment receipts - just make the payment online.
Submit the application. Only the last application submitted will be considered; earlier duplicate applications will be ignored.
Note down your application number for future reference.
Appear for written/typing/driving test as per the schedule communicated through your registered email/mobile.
Important: The authority will not be responsible for any technical issues during fee payment. Candidates should test payment via SBI before the deadline.

content_sections.text: Backlog Vacancies - 5 Posts Blind and visually impaired Current Vacancies - 1 Post Backlog Vacancies - 1 Post Blind and visually impaired Current Vacancies - 6 Posts Sub-total for Notification 429: 22 posts (backlog + current) Notes: Blind and visually impaired Mobile physically disabled, leprosy cured, dwarf, acid attack survivors, multiple disabilities Hearing impaired and speech impaired # Among SC (Cat-C) - the 59 most backward sub-castes; if unavailable, other Cat-C castes Sub-total for Notification 428: 88 posts (7 Drivers + 81 Peons)

content_sections.heading: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the educational requirements can refer to the post-wise breakdown below. Candidates must be at least 18 years old and must not exceed the maximum age on the last date of application. Age Relaxation: Government servants (State/Central/Public Sector) serving for at least 8 of the last 10 years in a lower post are eligible for relaxation up to the prescribed upper age limit. Ex-Servicemen: 3 additional years on top of military service period. Physically disabled candidates: 10 years relaxation. Widows: 10 years relaxation. As per Government Order No. SAASUI 262, Sec. 2025, dated 29.01.2026, 05 additional years are added to the upper age limit.

rows.qualification: (1) Second PUC pass (Pre-University Board) OR 3-year Diploma in Commercial Practice; OR current student; AND (2) Senior grade pass in Kannada & English Typing and Shorthand from Public Education Dept, OR Diploma in Commercial Practice, OR current student

SSLC pass or currently studying; Must hold a Heavy Motor Vehicle Driving Licence
SSLC pass or currently studying; Vehicle driving licence is preferred but not mandatory

content_sections.heading: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026 is merit-based and includes a written test followed by a skill/interview round. Candidates should prepare early as competition will be high given the large number of applicants expected. Written Test - Based on marks scored in the qualifying educational examination Typing Test - Type a passage of 120 words in 1 minute in both Kannada and English; transcribe it and type at 45 WPM (minimum 50 marks required to qualify); maximum 100 marks Interview / Oral Test - Marks scored in the interview round Final merit list based on aggregate of all three components Written Test - Based on qualifying exam marks Typing Test - Type a passage in Kannada and English (maximum 100 marks; minimum 50 marks to qualify) Interview / Oral Test Final merit based on aggregate Eligible candidates (50 marks in qualifying exam) called for driving test Shortlisting ratio: 1:25 per vacancy based on qualifying exam marks Interview carries 10 marks Shortlisting ratio: 1:25 per vacancy based on SSLC marks Interview carries 10 marks Shortlisting ratio: 1:10 per vacancy based on SSLC marks Interview carries 10 marks Probation Period: All selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years from the date of joining, extendable until satisfactory completion. Tie-breaking rule: If two or more candidates score equally, seniority in age (older person ranks higher) and the more qualified candidate ranks above less qualified.

content_sections.heading: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply - Step-by-Step Guide
content_sections.text: Candidates must apply exclusively through the online portal. No physical or offline applications will be accepted. Here is a clear step-by-step process: Visit the official recruitment portal: Click Here Read the full notification carefully before filling the form. Register with a valid email ID and mobile number (these will be used for all future communication). Fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details. Upload required documents - self-attested scanned copies of certificates and photographs. Pay the application fee online via SBI Collect (Net Banking / Debit / Credit Card). SC/ST/Cat-I/PwD candidates are fee-exempt. Do not upload fee payment receipts - just make the payment online. Submit the application. Only the last application submitted will be considered; earlier duplicate applications will be ignored. Note down your application number for future reference. Appear for written/typing/driving test as per the schedule communicated through your registered email/mobile. Important: The authority will not be responsible for any technical issues during fee payment. Candidates should test payment via SBI before the deadline.

content_sections.heading: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 - Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates shortlisted for document verification must bring originals and self-attested copies of: SSLC / PUC marksheets and certificates (originals for current students: birth certificate or SSLC) Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/Category-I/II/III) issued by the Karnataka Government Disability certificate from competent authority (for PwD candidates) Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate or release certificate from Zilla Sainik Board Rural candidate certificate in Karnataka Government format Transgender certificate under Section 6 of the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 Project-displaced / Scheme-nominee certificate as per Government Order Conduct certificate from School/College (valid for 2 years, with two gazetted officer endorsements) Government servant: NOC from the employing department Identity proof: Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, or other valid government photo ID

content_sections.heading: Bengaluru City Civil Court Recruitment 2026 - Troubleshooting & Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Even well-prepared candidates sometimes make avoidable errors. Here are the most common pitfalls: Incorrect name on fee payment: The fee must be paid only in the name of "Vitteeshadhikariyavaru, Nagar Civil Nyayalaya, Bengaluru." Payment in any other name will be rejected and the application disqualified. Paying fee via Demand Draft / Cheque / Money Order / Cash: Only online payment through SBI Collect is accepted. All other modes are invalid. Uploading the fee receipt: Do not upload the fee payment receipt in the application - simply make the payment. Submitting multiple applications: Only the last submitted application will be processed. Earlier submissions are automatically ignored - there is no benefit to submitting multiple applications. Wrong category certificate: Candidates claiming reservation must submit certificates issued specifically by the Karnataka Government in the prescribed format. Certificates from other states or unapproved formats will be rejected. Not providing correct email/mobile: All communication - including call letters for tests - will go only to the email and mobile number registered in the application. Wrong details mean missing the exam call. Missing the application window: The portal opens on 1 July 2026 and closes on 30 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. There is no provision for late or offline submission. Not reading category footnotes: Several vacancies in the Peon (Javana) category have special conditions - for example, the SC (Cat-C) backlog position is reserved for the 59 most backward sub-castes first; if unavailable, it reverts to other Cat-C castes. Conductor certificate for Driver: Drivers must produce a valid Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) licence. A light vehicle licence alone will not be sufficient. Submitting false information: Any false or incomplete information submitted, if discovered during or after selection, will lead to cancellation of candidature and may result in criminal prosecution.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 30 July 2026 by 11:59 PM. The fee payment deadline is also 30 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. No applications will be accepted after this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Across both notifications (429/2026 and 428/2026), there are over 106 vacancies covering Typist Grade-III, Peon (Attender), Driver, Process Server, and Peon (Javana) posts.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: General Merit candidates must pay ₹300/-. Category-II(A), II(B), III(A), III(B) candidates pay ₹150/-. SC, ST, Category-I, and Persons with Disability candidates are fully exempted from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q4. What is the official website to apply for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted only at https://bengaluru.dcourts.gov.in/recruitment-status-ccc/. No other website or offline method is valid.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for the Typist Grade-III post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed the Second PUC or hold a 3-year Diploma in Commercial Practice, along with a Senior Grade pass in Kannada and English Typing and Shorthand from the Public Education Department (or an equivalent Diploma), or be a current student in these courses.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for applying to Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be at least 18 years old. The upper age limit is 40 years for General Merit, 43 years for OBC (Category-II/III), and 45 years for SC/ST/Category-I candidates, as on the last date of application.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Bengaluru Civil Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 30 July 2026 by 11:59 PM. The fee payment deadline is also 30 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. No applications will be accepted after this date.
